title: trifolium procumbens subsp. campestre
intro: Trifolium procumbens subsp. campestre, also known as creeping white clover, is a subspecies of Trifolium procumbens that belongs to the legume family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Trifolium procumbens subsp. campestre include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Trifolium procumbens subsp. campestre can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
